h0: Break-of-structure long-dip setups have non-positive cost-adjusted expectancy out-of-sample.
h1: BOS long-dip setups deliver a positive cost-adjusted edge whose annualised PSR lower bound exceeds 0.95 over a >=1y out-of-sample window.
rationale: C4 block-permutation already scores BOS structure significance; the registered edge claim ties that to a PSR-positive forward window so the gate's psr_minimum check is the falsifier.

h0: Order-block reaction entries have non-positive cost-adjusted expectancy out-of-sample.
h1: OB entries deliver a positive cost-adjusted edge whose annualised PSR lower bound exceeds 0.95 over a >=1y out-of-sample window.
rationale: C4 permutation test covers OB edge significance; PSR lower bound is the primary forward-test falsifier consumed by the gate.

h0: Fair-value-gap fill setups have non-positive cost-adjusted expectancy out-of-sample.
h1: FVG setups deliver a positive cost-adjusted edge whose annualised PSR lower bound exceeds 0.95 over a >=1y out-of-sample window.
rationale: C6 already uses a probabilistic-Sharpe lower bound on FVG TRL; this registers PSR as the frozen primary metric so MinTRL/PSR jointly falsify the claim.

h0: Liquidity-sweep reversal setups have non-positive cost-adjusted expectancy out-of-sample after capacity/slippage.
h1: SWEEP setups deliver a positive cost-adjusted edge whose annualised PSR lower bound exceeds 0.95 over a >=1y out-of-sample window, net of Almgren-Chriss slippage.
benchmark: regime-matched SPY buy-and-hold, Almgren-Chriss slippage model
rationale: Sweep setups are the most liquidity-sensitive family; the hypothesis explicitly requires the edge to survive realistic slippage, not mid-price fills.
